steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: When you see your teacher in the morning, what do you say?
questions.hint: The sun is coming up. It's time for school!

questions.question_text: Your friend waves at you and says 'Hi!' What do you say back?
questions.hint: You want to be friendly. Say the same thing back!

questions.question_text: It's time to go home. What do you say to your classmates?
questions.hint: You're leaving. This word means 'see you later'!

items.definition: A greeting word
A casual greeting
To reply to a question
To hear attentively
A friendly greeting when you meet someone
A short way to say hello
What you say when someone asks a question
Use your ears to hear carefully

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 👋

steps.title: First Day Greeting
steps.text: He says, 'Hello!' She says, 'Hi!' They listen and answer the teacher.

steps.explanation: This is how you tell someone your name when you meet them.

items.explanation: Use 'is' with he, she, or it
Use 'your' to show something belongs to you
Add 's' for more than one friend
This is correct! We use 'is' with 'who' and 'she'.

items.question: ___ is your friend?
items.explanation: Use 'Who' to ask about a person

items.question: ___ is she?
items.explanation: Use 'Who' to ask about a person's identity

steps.audio_text: Good morning, class! Let me tell you a story. Tom sees his friend Lucy at school. He waves and says, 'Hello, Lucy!' Lucy smiles and says, 'Hi, Tom!' Their teacher asks, 'Who said hello first?' Tom answers, 'I did!' Everyone listens carefully.

steps.prompt: Let's practice speaking! Say each sentence clearly.

prompts.prompt: Say: I listen to my teacher.

questions.question_text: What do you say when you meet someone?
Who ___ she?
Which word means to use your ears carefully?
True or False: 'Hi' is a short way to say 'hello'.
When someone asks you a question, what do you give?

steps.instruction: Let's meet some new friends! Answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you say when you meet a new friend?
questions.hint: We say this when we first see someone.

questions.question_text: Who are the two new friends in our story?
questions.hint: Look at the picture. What are their names?

questions.question_text: What can friends do together?
questions.hint: Friends like to have fun with each other!

items.definition: Used to refer to two or more people
What someone is called
Not old or not known before
Having existed for a long time

items.hint: Not new, had it for a long time

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 👥

steps.title: Ben and Kim
steps.text: Today, Ben and Kim join our class. They are new. Ben is happy. Kim is shy.

steps.rule_pattern: ___ is my friend. ___ are my friends.
steps.explanation: Use 'He' or 'She' for one friend, and 'They' for two or more friends.

items.sentence: They is called Ben and Kim.

steps.audio_text: Hello, children! Today we have two new friends in our class. They are called Ben and Kim. Ben is seven years old. Kim is six years old. They are very happy to be here!

questions.question: How many new friends are there?

steps.prompt: Practice talking about friends! Say these sentences clearly.

prompts.prompt: Say: He is my new friend.
Say: They are my old friends.

questions.question_text: What does 'new' mean?
He ___ my new friend.
___ are Ben and Kim.
Read: 'She is called Kim. She is new.' Who is new?
True or False: 'They' is used for two or more people.

steps.instruction: Let's sing the alphabet song together! Then answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What letter comes after A?
Which letter looks like a circle?
What is the last letter of the alphabet?
questions.hint: It comes after Y at the end!

items.definition: A set of letters
Say letters in order
A symbol in words
A group of letters

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 🔤

steps.title: The Spelling Game
steps.text: In class, they play a spelling game. She spells 'dog'. He spells 'cat'. They clap for the winner!

steps.explanation: We use 'This is' to show or point to something.

items.question: ___ are happy children.
items.explanation: Use 'They' for more than one person

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Today we are learning the alphabet. Can you spell your name? Let's practice writing letters together. First, let's say all the letters from A to Z!

questions.question: What are the children learning today?
What does the teacher want children to spell?
What will they say from A to Z?

steps.prompt: Let's practice speaking! Listen and repeat these sentences.

prompts.prompt: Say: She is learning the alphabet.
Say: He is spelling a word.
Say: Who is writing the letter?

questions.question_text: What do we call A, B, C, D, E, F, G...?
She ___ learning letters.
When you write L-E-T-T-E-R, you ___ the word.
True or False: 'They is happy' is correct.
Which sentence is correct?

steps.instruction: Let's review what we learned! Answer these questions!

questions.question_text: When you meet someone for the first time, what do you say?
questions.hint: We use this word to greet people and make new friends!

questions.question_text: What do you say when someone asks 'What's your name?'
questions.hint: You tell them what people call you!

questions.question_text: When it's time to leave your friend, what do you say?
questions.hint: We say this when we're going home or leaving.

items.definition: A friendly word we say when we meet someone
Use your ears to hear carefully
What people call you
Something you just got or just started
Something from before, not new
Say the letters of a word one by one
One part of the alphabet like A, B, or C
Letters put together that mean something
A short, friendly greeting
What you say when someone asks a question
A word for other people or things
All the letters from A to Z
Pay attention with your ears
Say each letter in a word
What we call a person
A group of letters that means something

items.question: When you meet a friend, you say '___' to greet them.
When your teacher talks, you need to ___ carefully.
Your ___ is what people call you.
A toy you got today is ___.
When someone asks 'What's your name?', you ___.
A, B, C, D are all ___ in the alphabet.
All the letters from A to Z make the ___.
When you say C-A-T, you ___ the word cat.

steps.title: New Friends
steps.text: They are new. Hello! My name is Tim. Who is she? She is Ana. Let's listen and answer.

steps.rule_pattern: Who is ___? He is... / She is... / They are...
steps.explanation: We use 'he' for boys, 'she' for girls, and 'they' for more than one person.
examples: Who is Tom? He is my friend.
Who is Emma? She is my teacher.
Who are Sam and Lucy? They are my classmates.

items.explanation: After 'can' use the base form of the verb

items.sentence: The letter are on the table.

items.question: ___ are my classmates.
items.explanation: Use 'They' for more than one person

steps.audio_text: Good morning, class! Today we have two new students. They are Lucy and Ben. Please listen carefully when they spell their names. Lucy will spell her name first, then Ben will answer questions about the alphabet.

questions.question: How many new students are there?

steps.prompt: Let's practice speaking! Say these sentences about yourself and your friends.

prompts.prompt: Introduce yourself. Say: Hello, my name is...
Tell me about a friend. Say: He is... or She is...
prompts.expected_text: He is my friend. / She is my friend.

prompts.prompt: Tell me about two friends. Say: They are...

questions.question_text: When you use your ears carefully, you ___.
___ is my new friend. (talking about a boy)
Which word means all the letters from A to Z?
Read: 'Tom and Lucy are new students. They like to spell words.' Who are new students?
True or False: We say 'They is' when talking about two people.
